** Anesthesia services and anesthesia specialists are excluded.
All of British Columbia's Longitudinal Family Physician (LFP) payment model services and payments are grouped in Miscellaneous services — Other identified.

Outputs in the National Physician Database — Data Release reflect the clinical payments to physicians and service utilization for the fiscal years in question and do not reflect more recent agreements, contracts or programs that may be in place.
Based on gross direct fee-for-service payments. Reciprocal billing payments are not included.

The 2023–2024 Ontario results do not accurately reflect total clinical payments made to physicians. Several factors have affected the results, leading to an under-representation of total clinical payments. Factors include missing retroactive payments made to physicians (2.8%) and inaccurate counting of certain payments and premiums. The Ontario government and CIHI are working closely together to update the historical data and ensure accuracy on physician compensation results going forward.

Alternative forms of reimbursement, such as salary and capitation, are not included. Fee-for-service expenditure's proportion of total physician compensation is different in each jurisdiction; therefore, comparisons across jurisdictions should be made with caution (see Table A.2.2 and/or Table A.2.3 of National Physician Database — Payments Data, 2022–2023 for the proportions).
National Physician Database, Canadian Institute for Health Information.
